title: Answer the Buyer: A Sales Engineer Copilot
one_line: Build a retrieval copilot that answers technical buyer questions from product, security, and integration documents, with citations and honest gaps.
why_it_matters: Sales engineers answer the same technical questions across security reviews, integration scoping, and product depth, and a wrong answer in a security questionnaire erodes trust or kills a deal. A copilot that answers from the real documents with citations speeds the cycle, but only if it refuses to guess when the documents do not cover the question.
persona: Sales engineer at an enterprise SaaS company
current_workflow: The sales engineer searches scattered product docs, security policies, and integration guides, copies relevant passages into an answer, and checks each claim against the source before sending.
ai_workflow: The copilot retrieves passages from the product, security, and integration corpus, drafts an answer grounded in the retrieved text, and attaches a citation to every claim. When the corpus does not contain the answer, it says so and points to the nearest relevant material rather than inventing a response.
outputs: an explicit not-covered response when applicable
definition_of_done: On a synthetic question set that includes questions the corpus cannot answer, every answer carries a citation traceable to the source, unsupported questions return an explicit not-covered response, and no answer asserts a capability absent from the documents.
example_input: A buyer question: does the product support SCIM provisioning with Okta, and is data encrypted at rest?
example_output: Encryption at rest: yes, AES-256, cited to the security doc. SCIM with Okta: not covered in the current documents; the nearest reference is the SAML SSO guide. The copilot does not assert SCIM support.
